name: Coelacanth
conservationEfforts: CITES Appendix I listing bans trade. Research using submersibles monitors populations. Fishermen trained to release accidentally caught coelacanths.
description: A living fossil unchanged for 400 million years, once thought to have gone extinct with the dinosaurs until a living specimen was caught off South Africa in 1938. They give live birth and have lobed fins that move like legs.
